slides.title: A short nap can *reset your afternoon*.
slides.sub: The right nap restores alertness without stealing from night sleep. The trick is keeping it short and early.

slides.title: Short is the secret
slides.body: A nap of about twenty minutes *can refresh* you without leaving you groggy. Longer often backfires.

slides.title: When a nap helps
slides.body: On a short night or a slow afternoon, a brief rest *may support* focus and mood.

slides.title: Keep it early
slides.body: Aim for early afternoon. A nap too late in the day *can disturb* your night sleep.

slides.title: Do not over-rely
slides.body: Naps are a helpful top-up, not a fix for steady poor nights. Protect your real sleep first.

slides.title: Short, early, occasional
slides.body: Keep it brief, keep it early, and use it now and then. That *can support* steady energy.

slides.title: Rest in a way that *works*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, movement and steps and turns your rest into one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A nap done right can reset your whole afternoon. 🌿

Keep it short, around twenty minutes, and early in the day so it restores alertness without wrecking night sleep. Use it as a top-up, not a crutch. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your rest and daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How a Short Nap Restores Energy Without Hurting Sleep
